title: Score → result handoff: derive candidate_results from evaluation scores, then rank (closes the eval→results chain)
business_reason: OMR scoring (FR-0008) persists evaluation_candidate_scores; results ranking (FR-0006) ranks candidate_results — but nothing connected them (candidate_results had to be seeded directly). This handoff makes results_ops:generate populate candidate_results from the result batch's linked (scored) evaluation score batch, so the full chain runs from one trigger: responses → scores → candidate_results → ranking → certificate eligibility.

design.pure_helpers: server/results/resultHandoff.ts: answerKeyMaxScore(payload) (questions * marksCorrect) + scoreToResultRow({score, gradeCode, subjectCode, maxScore}) (computes percentage_score; sets generated/not_evaluated/v1) — both PURE + unit-tested.
design.handoff: handoffScoresToResults(supabase, resultBatchId): load result_batch.evaluation_score_batch_id → score_batch.answer_key_id → answer key (grade/subject/maxScore); copy evaluation_candidate_scores → candidate_results via the pure mapping; idempotent upsert on (result_batch_id, candidate_id, subject_code).
design.wiring: results_ops:generate effect runs the handoff as a PRELUDE when the result batch has no candidate_results yet, then ranks them (existing FR-0006 logic).

param_14.question: How do evaluation scores become ranked candidate results?
param_14.answer: On results_ops:generate, if the result batch has no candidate_results, the handoff copies its linked score batch's evaluation_candidate_scores into candidate_results (deriving grade/subject/max-score from the answer key and computing percentage), then the existing ranking + eligibility snapshot runs. Scores/percentages/ranks are all server-computed.
param_14.answer_source: results_ops/workflows.json (results_handoff_lifecycle), evaluation/result schemas, server/eval/scoring + ranking, FR-0006/0008
param_14.security_implications: candidate_results are derived server-side from approved-key scores, never client-supplied; idempotent upsert (no double rows); the handoff only runs when candidate_results are empty (won't clobber an existing/published batch); published-result immutability (FR-0006) still applies.
param_14.approval_implications: none new; downstream publish stays dual-approval.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: candidate_results.certificate_eligibility_status (from ranking) feeds certificate generation. Per-candidate grade from the student (vs the answer key's grade/subject) is a follow-up; correction-versioning deferred.
param_14.assumptions: one score batch per result batch; the answer key carries subject_code/grade_code + a parseable payload for max-score.
